Undergraduate & graduate totals

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage). Segment and funnel volumes are summed across all programs at this degree level for the primary reporting window.

Total undergraduate rolls up 47 programs (including undecided inquiry placeholders). In Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 the level generated 373,161 inquiries and 5,290 final enrollments (1.42% inquiry-to-enrollment). Metrics and charts below sum or weight across those programs.

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage). Segment and funnel volumes are summed across all programs at this degree level for the primary reporting window.

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage). Segment and funnel volumes are summed across all undecided inquiry-placeholder programs for the primary reporting window.

Undecided inquiry placeholders combines 9 undergraduate Salesforce programs used when a lead has not selected a degree-specific program. In Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 the group generated 52,460 inquiries and 836 final enrollments (1.59% inquiry-to-enrollment). Sankey and marketing-segment views below aggregate inquiry volume across these placeholders; per-program migration and enrolled-student profile are omitted.

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Business Administration generated 22,927 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 12% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#3 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 516 final enrollments (2.25% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (69.8% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (29.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 50%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 70% (below 80% for the level by 10 pts); Navigational 26% (above 18% for the level by 8 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 25% (below 38% for the level by 13 pts); Navigational 50% (above 44% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 25% (above 18% for the level by 8 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 6.1% of total undergraduate inquiries (22,927 of 373,161); 9.8% of level final enrollments (516 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.25% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.83 pts); B2B concentration 48% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 29.4% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (48.1% of 994 enrolled students; 478 in B2B); 16 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 16 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #1 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 994 matriculants, median age 34 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 51.5% female, 10 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (42.5%), 2 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (48.1%), 16 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 29.4% (11 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (31.1% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 47 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (425 stayed on-program; net +8.9% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Psychology generated 25,651 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 15% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#2 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 324 final enrollments (1.26% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (84.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (32.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 40%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 84% (above 80% for the level by 5 pts); Navigational 15% (below 18% for the level by 3 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 52% (above 38% for the level by 14 pts); Navigational 40% (below 44% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 8% (below 18% for the level by 10 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 6.9% of total undergraduate inquiries (25,651 of 373,161); 6.1% of level final enrollments (324 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.26% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.15 pts); Core concentration 56% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 56.4% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (56.4% of 525 enrolled students; 296 in Core); 16 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 16 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #1 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 525 matriculants, median age 31 is 2 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 76.9% female, 16 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (44.8%), 5 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to Core (56.4%), 16 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 56.4% (16 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (32.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 8 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (276 stayed on-program; net +2.6% of applied enrollments).

Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

AA in Business generated 29,240 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 384% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#1 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 322 final enrollments (1.10% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (82.6% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (44.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 41%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 83% (above 80% for the level by 3 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 43% (above 38% for the level by 5 pts); Navigational 41% (below 44% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 7.8% of total undergraduate inquiries (29,240 of 373,161); 6.1% of level final enrollments (322 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.10% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.32 pts); Core concentration 53% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 53.4% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (53.4% of 429 enrolled students; 229 in Core); 13 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 13 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #4 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 429 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 58.0% female, 3 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) (index 94 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is Black (39.2%), 7 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to Core (53.4%), 13 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 53.4% (13 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (36.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 4 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (241 stayed on-program; net +1.3% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Health Care Administration generated 19,903 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 7% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#5 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 285 final enrollments (1.43% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (80.8% of program leads); largest segment Display (43.4% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 45%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 31% (below 38% for the level by 7 pts); B2B 24% (above 18% for the level by 6 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 5.3% of total undergraduate inquiries (19,903 of 373,161); 5.4% of level final enrollments (285 of 5,290).

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (44.4% of 593 enrolled students; 263 in Core); 4 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 4 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #3 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 593 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 87.7% female, 26 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (43.5%), 11 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to Core (44.4%), 4 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 44.4% (4 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (30.9% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 33 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (253 stayed on-program; net +10.7% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology generated 13,078 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (flat vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#9 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 234 final enrollments (1.79% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (77.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (27.6% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 41%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: B2B 6% (above 3% for the level by 3 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Navigational 41% (below 44% for the level by 3 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 3.5% of total undergraduate inquiries (13,078 of 373,161); 4.4% of level final enrollments (234 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.79% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.37 pts); B2B concentration 38% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 27.7% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (38.1% of 404 enrolled students; 154 in B2B); 6 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 6 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #3 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 404 matriculants, median age 32 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 32.4% female, 29 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (36.4%), 4 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to B2B (38.1%), 6 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 27.7% (13 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (33.2% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 17 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (211 stayed on-program; net +7.1% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Social and Criminal Justice generated 22,862 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 33% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#4 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 224 final enrollments (0.98% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (82.4% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (36.1% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 36%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 56% (above 38% for the level by 18 pts); Navigational 36% (below 44% for the level by 8 pts); B2B 8% (below 18% for the level by 10 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 6.1% of total undergraduate inquiries (22,862 of 373,161); 4.2% of level final enrollments (224 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 0.98% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.44 pts); Core concentration 54% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 53.8% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (53.8% of 416 enrolled students; 224 in Core); 13 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 13 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #5 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 416 matriculants, median age 30 is 3 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 67.1% female, 6 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) (index 109 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is Black (39.7%), 7 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to Core (53.9%), 14 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 53.8% (13 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (29.3% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 17 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (192 stayed on-program; net +7.4% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Human Resources Management generated 7,530 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 14% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#14 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 187 final enrollments (2.48% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (72.2% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (25.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 47%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 72% (below 80% for the level by 7 pts); Navigational 23% (above 18% for the level by 5 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 28% (below 38% for the level by 10 pts); B2B 25% (above 18% for the level by 8 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 2.0% of total undergraduate inquiries (7,530 of 373,161); 3.5% of level final enrollments (187 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.48% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 1.07 pts); Core LOB share 32.4% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (34.8% of 293 enrolled students; 102 in B2B); 3 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 3 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #8 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 293 matriculants, median age 34 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 76.3% female, 15 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (36.9%), 3 pts below the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (34.8%), 3 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 32.4% (8 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (32.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 3 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (154 stayed on-program; net +1.7% of applied enrollments).

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Homeland Security and Emergency Management generated 19,768 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 72% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#6 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 165 final enrollments (0.83% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (90.0% of program leads); largest segment Display (60.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 46%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 90% (above 80% for the level by 10 pts); Navigational 10% (below 18% for the level by 8 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 51% (above 38% for the level by 13 pts); B2B 4% (below 18% for the level by 14 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 5.3% of total undergraduate inquiries (19,768 of 373,161); 3.1% of level final enrollments (165 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 0.83% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.58 pts); Military concentration 56% vs 21% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 26.9% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Military (56.2% of 201 enrolled students; 113 in Military); 35 pts above the total undergraduate share for Military (21.3%); 35 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Military (21.3%); top Military programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (178), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (126), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (117), BA in Homeland Security and Emergency Management (113), BA in Psychology (112); this program ranks #4 for Military.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 201 matriculants, median age 30 is 3 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 27.5% female, 34 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (49.8%), 10 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to Military (56.1%), 35 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Military (21.3%); Core share is 26.9% (14 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (30.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 7 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (138 stayed on-program; net +4.5% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

AA in Early Childhood Education generated 15,796 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (flat vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#8 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 158 final enrollments (1.00% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Business Leadership generated 10,259 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 10% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#10 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 145 final enrollments (1.41% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (82.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (39.3% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 50%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Navigational 14% (below 18% for the level by 3 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 24% (below 38% for the level by 14 pts); Navigational 50% (above 44% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 26% (above 18% for the level by 9 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 2.7% of total undergraduate inquiries (10,259 of 373,161); 2.7% of level final enrollments (145 of 5,290); B2B concentration 54% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 21.6% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (53.5% of 273 enrolled students; 146 in B2B); 22 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 22 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #4 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 273 matriculants, median age 37 is 4 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 49.3% female, 12 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (53.5%), 13 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (53.5%), 22 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 21.6% (19 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (30.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 12 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (119 stayed on-program; net +8.1% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Applied Behavioral Science generated 15,819 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 21% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#7 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 144 final enrollments (0.91% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (85.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (29.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 25%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 85% (above 80% for the level by 6 pts); Navigational 14% (below 18% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 67% (above 38% for the level by 28 pts); Navigational 25% (below 44% for the level by 19 pts); B2B 8% (below 18% for the level by 9 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 4.2% of total undergraduate inquiries (15,819 of 373,161); 2.7% of level final enrollments (144 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 0.91% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.51 pts); Core concentration 72% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 72.3% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (72.3% of 282 enrolled students; 204 in Core); 32 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 32 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #6 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 282 matriculants, median age 34 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 84.2% female, 23 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (39.7%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to Core (72.4%), 32 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 72.3% (32 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (36.2% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 35 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (126 stayed on-program; net +20.1% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Accounting generated 10,234 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 7% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#11 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 143 final enrollments (1.40% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (80.0% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (30.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 40%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Navigational 40% (below 44% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 2.7% of total undergraduate inquiries (10,234 of 373,161); 2.7% of level final enrollments (143 of 5,290); B2B concentration 42% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (41.8% of 232 enrolled students; 97 in B2B); 10 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 10 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #9 for B2B.

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Project Management generated 6,630 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (flat vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#18 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 136 final enrollments (2.05% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (78.2% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (32.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 55%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 30% (below 38% for the level by 8 pts); Navigational 55% (above 44% for the level by 11 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.8% of total undergraduate inquiries (6,630 of 373,161); 2.6% of level final enrollments (136 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.05% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.63 pts); B2B concentration 45% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 20.2% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (44.6% of 213 enrolled students; 95 in B2B); 13 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 13 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #10 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 213 matriculants, median age 34 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 34.0% female, 27 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (42.3%), 2 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (44.6%), 13 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 20.2% (20 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (31.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 1 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (106 stayed on-program; net -0.8% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Finance generated 5,896 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 12% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#19 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 123 final enrollments (2.09% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (75.9% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (31.1% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 49%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 76% (below 80% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 24% (below 38% for the level by 14 pts); Navigational 49% (above 44% for the level by 5 pts); B2B 27% (above 18% for the level by 9 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.6% of total undergraduate inquiries (5,896 of 373,161); 2.3% of level final enrollments (123 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.09% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.67 pts); B2B concentration 46% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 24.2% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (46.3% of 190 enrolled students; 88 in B2B); 14 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 14 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #12 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 190 matriculants, median age 28 is 5 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 43.4% female, 18 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (40.0%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (46.3%), 14 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 24.2% (16 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (28.4% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 4 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (102 stayed on-program; net +3.4% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Early Childhood Education generated 9,130 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (flat vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#12 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 116 final enrollments (1.27% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (80.0% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (25.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 43%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 55% (above 38% for the level by 16 pts); B2B 2% (below 18% for the level by 16 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 2.4% of total undergraduate inquiries (9,130 of 373,161); 2.2% of level final enrollments (116 of 5,290); Core concentration 69% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 69.0% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (69.0% of 187 enrolled students; 129 in Core); 29 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 29 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #8 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 187 matriculants, median age 30 is 3 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 90.9% female, 30 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (38.5%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to Core (69.1%), 29 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 69.0% (29 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (32.6% of matriculants).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Health and Human Services generated 6,926 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 127% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#17 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 115 final enrollments (1.66% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (80.3% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (44.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 40%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 43% (above 38% for the level by 4 pts); Navigational 40% (below 44% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.9% of total undergraduate inquiries (6,926 of 373,161); 2.2% of level final enrollments (115 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.66% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.24 pts); Core concentration 70% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 70.3% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (70.3% of 158 enrolled students; 111 in Core); 30 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 30 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #10 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 158 matriculants, median age 36 is 3 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 88.0% female, 27 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (53.3%), 21 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to Core (70.2%), 30 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 70.3% (30 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (46.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 7 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (91 stayed on-program; net -6.5% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Operations Management and Analysis generated 3,830 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 8% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#27 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 109 final enrollments (2.85% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (68.4% of program leads); largest segment Organic (26.3% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 47%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 68% (below 80% for the level by 11 pts); Navigational 24% (above 18% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 8% (above 3% for the level by 5 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 16% (below 38% for the level by 22 pts); B2B 37% (above 18% for the level by 19 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.0% of total undergraduate inquiries (3,830 of 373,161); 2.1% of level final enrollments (109 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.85% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 1.43 pts); B2B concentration 54% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 17.6% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (54.5% of 222 enrolled students; 121 in B2B); 22 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 22 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #5 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 222 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 37.8% female, 24 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (47.7%), 8 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (54.5%), 22 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 17.6% (23 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (32.9% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 3 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (91 stayed on-program; net +2.9% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BS in Information Technology generated 8,177 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 8% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#13 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 97 final enrollments (1.19% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (78.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (32.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 44%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 26% (below 38% for the level by 12 pts); B2B 30% (above 18% for the level by 12 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 2.2% of total undergraduate inquiries (8,177 of 373,161); 1.8% of level final enrollments (97 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.19% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.23 pts); B2B concentration 46% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 23.4% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (46.4% of 192 enrolled students; 89 in B2B); 14 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 14 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #11 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 192 matriculants, median age 33 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 29.8% female, 32 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (42.2%), 2 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (46.4%), 14 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 23.4% (17 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Marketing generated 7,186 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 9% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#15 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 88 final enrollments (1.22% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (79.0% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (32.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 44%.

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.9% of total undergraduate inquiries (7,186 of 373,161); 1.7% of level final enrollments (88 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.22% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.19 pts).

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (40.6% of 133 enrolled students; 54 in Core); in line with the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); in line with enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #16 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 133 matriculants, median age 29 is 4 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 55.6% female, 6 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) (index 91 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is White (45.1%), 5 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to Core (40.6%), in line with typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 40.6% (in line with undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (29.3% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 3 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (80 stayed on-program; net +3.3% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BS in Health Information Management generated 4,557 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 60% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#23 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 86 final enrollments (1.89% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (67.6% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (29.7% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 52%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 68% (below 80% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 26% (above 18% for the level by 8 pts); B2B 6% (above 3% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 20% (below 38% for the level by 18 pts); Navigational 52% (above 44% for the level by 8 pts); B2B 28% (above 18% for the level by 10 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.2% of total undergraduate inquiries (4,557 of 373,161); 1.6% of level final enrollments (86 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.89% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.47 pts); B2B concentration 42% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (42.4% of 139 enrolled students; 59 in B2B); 10 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 10 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #15 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 139 matriculants, median age 37 is 4 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 91.4% female, 30 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (46.0%), 13 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to B2B (42.4%), 10 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 37.4% (3 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (34.5% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 7 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (70 stayed on-program; net -8.9% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BS in Computer Software Technology generated 7,021 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 20% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#16 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 84 final enrollments (1.20% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (81.6% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (34.6% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 48%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Navigational 14% (below 18% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 34% (below 38% for the level by 4 pts); Navigational 48% (above 44% for the level by 3 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.9% of total undergraduate inquiries (7,021 of 373,161); 1.6% of level final enrollments (84 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.20% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.22 pts); B2B concentration 50% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 29.8% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (49.6% of 141 enrolled students; 70 in B2B); 18 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 18 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #14 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 141 matriculants, median age 30 is 3 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 27.7% female, 34 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (41.8%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (49.6%), 18 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 29.8% (11 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

AA in Organizational Management generated 5,553 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 451% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#20 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 82 final enrollments (1.48% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (72.7% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (26.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 44%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 73% (below 80% for the level by 7 pts); Navigational 24% (above 18% for the level by 6 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.5% of total undergraduate inquiries (5,553 of 373,161); 1.6% of level final enrollments (82 of 5,290).

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (41.5% of 123 enrolled students; 51 in Core); in line with the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); in line with enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #18 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 123 matriculants, median age 38 is 5 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 55.3% female, 6 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) (index 90 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is Black (39.0%), 6 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to Core (41.5%), in line with typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 41.5% (in line with undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (31.7% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 6 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (62 stayed on-program; net +7.2% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Organizational Management generated 2,996 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 34% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#28 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 71 final enrollments (2.37% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (67.5% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (34.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 58%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 68% (below 80% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 29% (above 18% for the level by 11 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 24% (below 38% for the level by 14 pts); Navigational 58% (above 44% for the level by 14 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.8% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,996 of 373,161); 1.3% of level final enrollments (71 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.37% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.95 pts); B2B concentration 51% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 10.9% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (51.3% of 230 enrolled students; 118 in B2B); 19 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 19 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #6 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 230 matriculants, median age 37 is 4 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 40.9% female, 20 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (53.1%), 13 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (51.3%), 19 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 10.9% (30 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (35.2% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 21 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (51 stayed on-program; net +25.0% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Education Studies generated 4,593 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 6% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#22 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 68 final enrollments (1.48% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (81.6% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (27.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 43%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 48% (above 38% for the level by 10 pts); B2B 9% (below 18% for the level by 9 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.2% of total undergraduate inquiries (4,593 of 373,161); 1.3% of level final enrollments (68 of 5,290); Core concentration 73% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 72.8% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (72.8% of 92 enrolled students; 67 in Core); 32 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 32 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #13 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 92 matriculants, median age 29 is 4 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 73.9% female, 13 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (39.1%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to Core (72.8%), 32 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 72.8% (32 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (39.1% of matriculants).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Sociology generated 4,254 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 10% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#26 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 56 final enrollments (1.32% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (79.4% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (30.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 43%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 46% (above 38% for the level by 8 pts); B2B 11% (below 18% for the level by 7 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.1% of total undergraduate inquiries (4,254 of 373,161); 1.1% of level final enrollments (56 of 5,290); Core concentration 59% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 58.7% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (58.7% of 75 enrolled students; 44 in Core); 18 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 18 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #22 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 75 matriculants, median age 32 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 76.0% female, 15 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (37.3%), 5 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to Core (58.6%), 18 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 58.7% (18 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (30.7% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 6 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (40 stayed on-program; net -14.0% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Liberal Arts generated 5,438 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 7% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#21 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 55 final enrollments (1.01% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (78.6% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (34.3% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 36%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 47% (above 38% for the level by 9 pts); Navigational 36% (below 44% for the level by 8 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.5% of total undergraduate inquiries (5,438 of 373,161); 1.0% of level final enrollments (55 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.01% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.41 pts); Core concentration 58% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 58.2% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (58.2% of 79 enrolled students; 46 in Core); 18 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 18 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #20 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 79 matriculants, median age 38 is 5 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 63.3% female, in line with the undergraduate share (61.4%) (index 103 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is White (41.8%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to Core (58.2%), 18 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 58.2% (18 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Business Information Systems generated 4,286 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 12% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#25 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 53 final enrollments (1.24% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (80.4% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (39.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 43%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 28% (below 38% for the level by 10 pts); B2B 28% (above 18% for the level by 11 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.1% of total undergraduate inquiries (4,286 of 373,161); 1.0% of level final enrollments (53 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.24% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.18 pts); B2B concentration 61% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 18.9% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (61.1% of 95 enrolled students; 58 in B2B); 29 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 29 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #16 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 95 matriculants, median age 34 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 42.1% female, 19 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (44.2%), 4 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (61.1%), 29 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 18.9% (22 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (29.5% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 3 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (42 stayed on-program; net -6.2% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Health and Wellness generated 4,549 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 90880% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#24 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 42 final enrollments (0.92% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (78.3% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (42.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 50%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Navigational 50% (above 44% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 12% (below 18% for the level by 6 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.2% of total undergraduate inquiries (4,549 of 373,161); 0.8% of level final enrollments (42 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 0.92% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.49 pts).

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (38.2% of 55 enrolled students; 21 in Core); 2 pts below the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 2 pts below enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #29 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 55 matriculants, median age 33 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 63.6% female, 2 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) (index 104 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is Black (36.4%), 4 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to Core (38.2%), 2 pts below typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 38.2% (2 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (40.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 6 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (39 stayed on-program; net +12.8% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Supply Chain Management generated 1,299 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (no prior-period baseline in Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking lower volume (#34 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 39 final enrollments (3.00% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (54.8% of program leads); largest segment Organic (36.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 51%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 55% (below 80% for the level by 25 pts); Navigational 36% (above 18% for the level by 18 pts); B2B 9% (above 3% for the level by 6 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 23% (below 38% for the level by 15 pts); Navigational 51% (above 44% for the level by 7 pts); B2B 26% (above 18% for the level by 8 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.3% of total undergraduate inquiries (1,299 of 373,161); 0.7% of level final enrollments (39 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 3.00% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 1.58 pts); B2B concentration 41% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 16.4% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (41.0% of 61 enrolled students; 25 in B2B); 9 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 9 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #21 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 61 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 31.1% female, 30 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (45.9%), 6 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (41.0%), 9 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 16.4% (24 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (29.5% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 1 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (35 stayed on-program; net +2.7% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BS in Nursing generated 2,332 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 15% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking lower volume (#31 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 37 final enrollments (1.59% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (43.3% of program leads); largest segment Organic (50.3% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 38%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 43% (below 80% for the level by 36 pts); Navigational 40% (above 18% for the level by 22 pts); B2B 17% (above 3% for the level by 14 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 14% (below 38% for the level by 25 pts); Navigational 38% (below 44% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 49% (above 18% for the level by 31 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.6% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,332 of 373,161); 0.7% of level final enrollments (37 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.59% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.17 pts); B2B concentration 93% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 3.6% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (92.9% of 56 enrolled students; 52 in B2B); 61 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 61 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #17 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 56 matriculants, median age 36 is 3 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 78.6% female, 17 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (55.4%), 15 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to B2B (92.8%), 61 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 3.6% (37 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Program summary

AA in Military Studies generated 1,244 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 253% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking lower volume (#35 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 27 final enrollments (2.17% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (75.3% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (44.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 70%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 75% (below 80% for the level by 4 pts); Navigational 24% (above 18% for the level by 6 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 30% (below 38% for the level by 9 pts); Navigational 70% (above 44% for the level by 26 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.3% of total undergraduate inquiries (1,244 of 373,161); 0.5% of level final enrollments (27 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.17% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.75 pts); Military concentration 78% vs 21% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 14.8% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Military (77.8% of 27 enrolled students; 21 in Military); 56 pts above the total undergraduate share for Military (21.3%); 56 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Military (21.3%); top Military programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (178), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (126), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (117), BA in Homeland Security and Emergency Management (113), BA in Psychology (112); this program ranks #22 for Military.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 27 matriculants, median age 28 is 5 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 18.5% female, 43 pts below the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (48.1%), 8 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to Military (77.8%), 56 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Military (21.3%); Core share is 14.8% (26 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (33.3% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 1 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (13 stayed on-program; net +6.2% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Early Childhood Education Administration generated 2,566 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 12% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#30 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 25 final enrollments (0.97% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (78.5% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (32.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 32%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 64% (above 38% for the level by 26 pts); Navigational 32% (below 44% for the level by 12 pts); B2B 4% (below 18% for the level by 14 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.7% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,566 of 373,161); 0.5% of level final enrollments (25 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 0.97% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.44 pts); Core concentration 53% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 52.9% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (52.9% of 34 enrolled students; 18 in Core); 12 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 12 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #31 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 34 matriculants, median age 36 is 3 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 97.1% female, 36 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (41.2%), 9 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to Core (53.0%), 13 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 52.9% (12 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (29.4% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 4 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (19 stayed on-program; net -19.0% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Communication Studies generated 1,014 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (no prior-period baseline in Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking lower volume (#37 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 19 final enrollments (1.87% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (66.0% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (35.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 53%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 66% (below 80% for the level by 14 pts); Navigational 30% (above 18% for the level by 12 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 32% (below 38% for the level by 7 pts); Navigational 53% (above 44% for the level by 8 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.3% of total undergraduate inquiries (1,014 of 373,161); 0.4% of level final enrollments (19 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.87% (above total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.46 pts); B2B concentration 48% vs 32% for all total undergraduate matriculations.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (48.1% of 27 enrolled students; 13 in B2B); 16 pts above the total undergraduate share for B2B (32.0%); 16 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (32.0%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (478), BA in Health Care Administration (208), BS in Cyber and Data Security Technology (154), BA in Business Leadership (146), BA in Operations Management and Analysis (121); this program ranks #26 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 27 matriculants, median age 32 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 81.5% female, 20 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (40.7%), 8 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (32.6%); line of business skews to B2B (48.1%), 16 pts above typical undergraduate mix for B2B (32.0%); Core share is 37.0% (3 pts below undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (29.6% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 3 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (15 stayed on-program; net +15.0% of applied enrollments).
