Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Early Childhood Development with Differentiated Instruction generated 2,867 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (down 38% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#29 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 17 final enrollments (0.59% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (80.5% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (48.4% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 53%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Navigational 53% (above 44% for the level by 9 pts); B2B 6% (below 18% for the level by 12 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.8% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,867 of 373,161); 0.3% of level final enrollments (17 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 0.59% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.82 pts); Core concentration 71% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 71.1% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (71.1% of 45 enrolled students; 32 in Core); 31 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 31 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #27 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 45 matriculants, median age 33 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 95.6% female, 34 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (48.9%), 9 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (40.1%); line of business skews to Core (71.2%), 31 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (40.4%); Core share is 71.1% (31 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (24.4% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 1 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (13 stayed on-program; net +5.6% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Social Science generated 531 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 53000% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking lower volume (#38 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 6 final enrollments (1.13% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.38%).

Lead sources: Paid (70.2% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (43.6% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 17%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 70% (below 80% for the level by 9 pts); Navigational 28% (above 18% for the level by 10 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 50% (above 38% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 17% (below 44% for the level by 28 pts); B2B 33% (above 18% for the level by 16 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.1% of total undergraduate inquiries (531 of 373,161); 0.1% of level final enrollments (6 of 5,290); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.13% (below total undergraduate 1.42% by 0.29 pts); Core concentration 50% vs 40% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 50.0% vs 40.4% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (50.0% of 4 enrolled students; 2 in Core); 10 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (40.4%); 10 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (40.4%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Psychology (296), BA in Business Administration (292), BA in Health Care Administration (263), AA in Business (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (224); this program ranks #37 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 4 matriculants, median age 37 is 4 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 75.0% female, 14 pts above the undergraduate share (61.4%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Other (50.0%), 34 pts above the undergraduate norm for Other (15.8%); line of business skews to Military (50.0%), 29 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Military (21.3%); Core share is 50.0% (10 pts above undergraduate Core at 40.4%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (50.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: shows no net 0 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (5 stayed on-program; net +0.0% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

Master of Business Administration generated 8,936 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 47% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#1 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 455 final enrollments (5.09% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (39.4% of program leads); largest segment Organic (51.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 40%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 39% (below 61% for the level by 22 pts); Navigational 39% (above 30% for the level by 9 pts); B2B 22% (above 9% for the level by 13 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 11% (below 22% for the level by 11 pts); Navigational 40% (below 45% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 50% (above 33% for the level by 16 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 16.7% of total graduate inquiries (8,936 of 53,512); 33.9% of level final enrollments (455 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 5.09% (above total graduate 2.51% by 2.58 pts); B2B concentration 81% vs 61% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 6.9% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (81.2% of 965 enrolled students; 784 in B2B); 20 pts above the total graduate share for B2B (60.9%); 20 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (60.9%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (784), MA in Health Care Administration (153), MA in Organizational Management (89), Master of Human Resource Management (60), Master of Information Systems Management (60); this program ranks #1 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 965 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts below the graduate average (37); 49.5% female, 12 pts below the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (49.0%), 6 pts above the graduate norm for White (42.8%); line of business skews to B2B (81.3%), 20 pts above typical graduate mix for B2B (60.9%); Core share is 6.9% (11 pts below graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (24.7% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 23 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (416 stayed on-program; net +5.2% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MA in Health Care Administration generated 5,895 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 94% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#3 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 128 final enrollments (2.17% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (70.8% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (30.6% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 48%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 71% (above 61% for the level by 10 pts); Navigational 22% (below 30% for the level by 8 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 11.0% of total graduate inquiries (5,895 of 53,512); 9.5% of level final enrollments (128 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.17% (below total graduate 2.51% by 0.34 pts); B2B concentration 73% vs 61% for all total graduate matriculations.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (73.2% of 209 enrolled students; 153 in B2B); 12 pts above the total graduate share for B2B (60.9%); 12 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (60.9%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (784), MA in Health Care Administration (153), MA in Organizational Management (89), Master of Human Resource Management (60), Master of Information Systems Management (60); this program ranks #2 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 209 matriculants, median age 39 is 2 pts above the graduate average (37); 81.7% female, 20 pts above the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (44.0%), in line with the graduate norm for White (42.8%); line of business skews to B2B (73.2%), 12 pts above typical graduate mix for B2B (60.9%); Core share is 13.9% (4 pts below graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Northeast (34.9% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 7 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (103 stayed on-program; net +6.2% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MA in Psychology generated 6,258 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 30% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#2 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 114 final enrollments (1.82% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (69.9% of program leads); largest segment Display (24.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 45%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 70% (above 61% for the level by 9 pts); Navigational 26% (below 30% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 4% (below 9% for the level by 5 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 47% (above 22% for the level by 26 pts); B2B 8% (below 33% for the level by 25 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 11.7% of total graduate inquiries (6,258 of 53,512); 8.5% of level final enrollments (114 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.82% (below total graduate 2.51% by 0.69 pts); Core concentration 49% vs 18% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 48.7% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (48.7% of 150 enrolled students; 73 in Core); 31 pts above the total graduate share for Core (17.9%); 31 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (17.9%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: MA in Psychology (73), Master of Business Administration (67), MA in Human Services (40), MA in Education (32), MA in Health Care Administration (29); this program ranks #1 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 150 matriculants, median age 38 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 76.4% female, 15 pts above the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (42.0%), in line with the graduate norm for White (42.8%); line of business skews to Core (48.7%), 31 pts above typical graduate mix for Core (17.9%); Core share is 48.7% (31 pts above graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (33.3% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 1 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (85 stayed on-program; net -1.1% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MA in Organizational Management generated 1,786 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 30% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#11 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 87 final enrollments (4.87% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Navigational (51.6% of program leads); largest segment Organic (52.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 52%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 35% (below 61% for the level by 26 pts); Navigational 52% (above 30% for the level by 22 pts); B2B 14% (above 9% for the level by 5 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 18% (below 22% for the level by 3 pts); Navigational 52% (above 45% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 30% (below 33% for the level by 3 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 3.3% of total graduate inquiries (1,786 of 53,512); 6.5% of level final enrollments (87 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 4.87% (above total graduate 2.51% by 2.36 pts); B2B concentration 45% vs 61% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 10.1% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (44.9% of 198 enrolled students; 89 in B2B); 16 pts below the total graduate share for B2B (60.9%); 16 pts below enrolled-student baseline for B2B (60.9%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (784), MA in Health Care Administration (153), MA in Organizational Management (89), Master of Human Resource Management (60), Master of Information Systems Management (60); this program ranks #3 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 198 matriculants, median age 42 is 5 pts above the graduate average (37); 63.9% female, 3 pts above the graduate share (61.3%) (index 104 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is White (39.4%), 3 pts below the graduate norm for White (42.8%); line of business skews to B2B (44.9%), 16 pts below typical graduate mix for B2B (60.9%); Core share is 10.1% (8 pts below graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (31.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 8 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (78 stayed on-program; net +9.1% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

Master of Human Resource Management generated 2,376 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 18% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#8 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 74 final enrollments (3.11% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (53.9% of program leads); largest segment Organic (35.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 51%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 54% (below 61% for the level by 7 pts); Navigational 38% (above 30% for the level by 8 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 12% (below 22% for the level by 9 pts); Navigational 51% (above 45% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 36% (above 33% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 4.4% of total graduate inquiries (2,376 of 53,512); 5.5% of level final enrollments (74 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 3.11% (above total graduate 2.51% by 0.60 pts); B2B concentration 49% vs 61% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 23.8% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (49.2% of 122 enrolled students; 60 in B2B); 12 pts below the total graduate share for B2B (60.9%); 12 pts below enrolled-student baseline for B2B (60.9%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (784), MA in Health Care Administration (153), MA in Organizational Management (89), Master of Human Resource Management (60), Master of Information Systems Management (60); this program ranks #4 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 122 matriculants, median age 36 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 74.4% female, 13 pts above the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (41.0%), in line with the graduate norm for White (42.8%); line of business skews to B2B (49.2%), 12 pts below typical graduate mix for B2B (60.9%); Core share is 23.8% (6 pts above graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (32.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 3 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (59 stayed on-program; net -4.8% of applied enrollments).

MS in Criminal Justice generated 5,262 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 58% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking top-tier (#4 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 55 final enrollments (1.05% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (79.2% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (28.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 44%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 79% (above 61% for the level by 18 pts); Navigational 19% (below 30% for the level by 11 pts); B2B 1% (below 9% for the level by 8 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 51% (above 22% for the level by 29 pts); B2B 6% (below 33% for the level by 28 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 9.8% of total graduate inquiries (5,262 of 53,512); 4.1% of level final enrollments (55 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.05% (below total graduate 2.51% by 1.46 pts); Core concentration 34% vs 18% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 34.5% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (34.5% of 58 enrolled students; 20 in Core); 17 pts above the total graduate share for Core (17.9%); 17 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (17.9%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: MA in Psychology (73), Master of Business Administration (67), MA in Human Services (40), MA in Education (32), MA in Health Care Administration (29); this program ranks #8 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 58 matriculants, median age 36 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 56.9% female, 4 pts below the graduate share (61.3%) (index 93 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is White (37.9%), 5 pts below the graduate norm for White (42.8%); line of business skews to Military (34.5%), 20 pts above typical graduate mix for Military (14.8%); Core share is 34.5% (17 pts above graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (36.2% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 2 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (30 stayed on-program; net -6.7% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

MA in Human Services generated 3,164 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 80% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#5 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 52 final enrollments (1.64% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (70.9% of program leads); largest segment Display (33.3% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 62%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 71% (above 61% for the level by 10 pts); Navigational 26% (below 30% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 3% (below 9% for the level by 6 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 33% (above 22% for the level by 11 pts); Navigational 62% (above 45% for the level by 16 pts); B2B 6% (below 33% for the level by 27 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 5.9% of total graduate inquiries (3,164 of 53,512); 3.9% of level final enrollments (52 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.64% (below total graduate 2.51% by 0.87 pts); Core concentration 52% vs 18% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 51.9% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (51.9% of 77 enrolled students; 40 in Core); 34 pts above the total graduate share for Core (17.9%); 34 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (17.9%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: MA in Psychology (73), Master of Business Administration (67), MA in Human Services (40), MA in Education (32), MA in Health Care Administration (29); this program ranks #3 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 77 matriculants, median age 38 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 85.7% female, 24 pts above the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (55.8%), 28 pts above the graduate norm for Black (27.3%); line of business skews to Core (51.9%), 34 pts above typical graduate mix for Core (17.9%); Core share is 51.9% (34 pts above graduate Core at 17.9%). The program recorded 47 final enrollments (1.70% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MS in Health Informatics and Analytics generated 2,158 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 64% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#9 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 46 final enrollments (2.13% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (60.2% of program leads); largest segment Organic (32.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 39%. Versus total graduate lead mix: B2B 12% (above 9% for the level by 3 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 15% (below 22% for the level by 6 pts); Navigational 39% (below 45% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 46% (above 33% for the level by 13 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 4.0% of total graduate inquiries (2,158 of 53,512); 3.4% of level final enrollments (46 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.13% (below total graduate 2.51% by 0.38 pts); B2B concentration 70% vs 61% for all total graduate matriculations.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (70.0% of 80 enrolled students; 56 in B2B); 9 pts above the total graduate share for B2B (60.9%); 9 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (60.9%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (784), MA in Health Care Administration (153), MA in Organizational Management (89), Master of Human Resource Management (60), Master of Information Systems Management (60); this program ranks #6 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 80 matriculants, median age 36 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 77.5% female, 16 pts above the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (40.0%), 13 pts above the graduate norm for Black (27.3%); line of business skews to B2B (70.1%), 9 pts above typical graduate mix for B2B (60.9%); Core share is 16.2% (in line with graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Northeast (36.2% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 1 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (39 stayed on-program; net +2.4% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

Master of Information Systems Management generated 1,759 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 34% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#12 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 37 final enrollments (2.10% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (54.2% of program leads); largest segment Organic (37.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 51%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 54% (below 61% for the level by 7 pts); B2B 15% (above 9% for the level by 6 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 14% (below 22% for the level by 8 pts); Navigational 51% (above 45% for the level by 6 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 3.3% of total graduate inquiries (1,759 of 53,512); 2.8% of level final enrollments (37 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.10% (below total graduate 2.51% by 0.41 pts); B2B concentration 67% vs 61% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 10.0% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (66.7% of 90 enrolled students; 60 in B2B); 6 pts above the total graduate share for B2B (60.9%); 6 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (60.9%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (784), MA in Health Care Administration (153), MA in Organizational Management (89), Master of Human Resource Management (60), Master of Information Systems Management (60); this program ranks #5 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 90 matriculants, median age 38 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 35.2% female, 26 pts below the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (38.9%), 4 pts below the graduate norm for White (42.8%); line of business skews to B2B (66.6%), 6 pts above typical graduate mix for B2B (60.9%); Core share is 10.0% (8 pts below graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (32.2% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 2 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (30 stayed on-program; net +5.6% of applied enrollments).

Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MS in Technology Management generated 1,402 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 59% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#15 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 36 final enrollments (2.57% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (55.6% of program leads); largest segment Organic (34.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 53%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 56% (below 61% for the level by 5 pts); B2B 13% (above 9% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 17% (below 22% for the level by 5 pts); Navigational 53% (above 45% for the level by 7 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 2.6% of total graduate inquiries (1,402 of 53,512); 2.7% of level final enrollments (36 of 1,343); B2B concentration 72% vs 61% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 10.6% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (72.3% of 47 enrolled students; 34 in B2B); 11 pts above the total graduate share for B2B (60.9%); 11 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (60.9%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (784), MA in Health Care Administration (153), MA in Organizational Management (89), Master of Human Resource Management (60), Master of Information Systems Management (60); this program ranks #7 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 47 matriculants, median age 39 is 2 pts above the graduate average (37); 31.9% female, 29 pts below the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (40.4%), 2 pts below the graduate norm for White (42.8%); line of business skews to B2B (72.4%), 12 pts above typical graduate mix for B2B (60.9%); Core share is 10.6% (7 pts below graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (31.9% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 5 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (23 stayed on-program; net -18.5% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

MA in Education generated 2,654 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 15% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#7 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 34 final enrollments (1.28% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (68.4% of program leads); largest segment Display (27.1% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 44%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 68% (above 61% for the level by 8 pts); B2B 2% (below 9% for the level by 7 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 47% (above 22% for the level by 26 pts); B2B 9% (below 33% for the level by 24 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 5.0% of total graduate inquiries (2,654 of 53,512); 2.5% of level final enrollments (34 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.28% (below total graduate 2.51% by 1.23 pts); Core concentration 53% vs 18% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 53.3% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (53.3% of 60 enrolled students; 32 in Core); 35 pts above the total graduate share for Core (17.9%); 35 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (17.9%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: MA in Psychology (73), Master of Business Administration (67), MA in Human Services (40), MA in Education (32), MA in Health Care Administration (29); this program ranks #4 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 60 matriculants, median age 41 is 4 pts above the graduate average (37); 76.3% female, 15 pts above the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (38.3%), 11 pts above the graduate norm for Black (27.3%); line of business skews to Core (53.3%), 35 pts above typical graduate mix for Core (17.9%); Core share is 53.3% (35 pts above graduate Core at 17.9%). Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MA in Early Childhood Education Leadership generated 1,247 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 32% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#16 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 32 final enrollments (2.57% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (56.3% of program leads); largest segment Organic (30.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 78%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 56% (below 61% for the level by 5 pts); Navigational 42% (above 30% for the level by 12 pts); B2B 2% (below 9% for the level by 7 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Navigational 78% (above 45% for the level by 33 pts); B2B 3% (below 33% for the level by 30 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 2.3% of total graduate inquiries (1,247 of 53,512); 2.4% of level final enrollments (32 of 1,343); Military concentration 40% vs 15% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 37.8% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Military (40.5% of 37 enrolled students; 15 in Military); 26 pts above the total graduate share for Military (14.8%); 26 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Military (14.8%); top Military programs by gross matriculations: MA in Organizational Management (76), Master of Business Administration (74), MA in Psychology (30), Master of Human Resource Management (28), MS in Criminal Justice (20); this program ranks #7 for Military.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 37 matriculants, median age 39 is 2 pts above the graduate average (37); 86.5% female, 25 pts above the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (51.4%), 24 pts above the graduate norm for Black (27.3%); line of business skews to Military (40.5%), 26 pts above typical graduate mix for Military (14.8%); Core share is 37.8% (20 pts above graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (37.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 6 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (25 stayed on-program; net -24.0% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MS in Finance generated 1,437 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 71% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#13 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 29 final enrollments (2.02% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (56.5% of program leads); largest segment Organic (31.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 31%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 56% (below 61% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 14% (above 9% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Navigational 31% (below 45% for the level by 14 pts); B2B 48% (above 33% for the level by 15 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 2.7% of total graduate inquiries (1,437 of 53,512); 2.2% of level final enrollments (29 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.02% (below total graduate 2.51% by 0.49 pts); B2B concentration 76% vs 61% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 8.8% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (76.5% of 34 enrolled students; 26 in B2B); 16 pts above the total graduate share for B2B (60.9%); 16 pts above enrolled-student baseline for B2B (60.9%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (784), MA in Health Care Administration (153), MA in Organizational Management (89), Master of Human Resource Management (60), Master of Information Systems Management (60); this program ranks #10 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 34 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts below the graduate average (37); 47.1% female, 14 pts below the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (50.0%), 7 pts above the graduate norm for White (42.8%); line of business skews to B2B (76.5%), 16 pts above typical graduate mix for B2B (60.9%); Core share is 8.8% (9 pts below graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (26.5% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 1 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (23 stayed on-program; net -4.2% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

Master of Accountancy generated 1,416 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 110% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking below median (#14 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 26 final enrollments (1.84% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (55.4% of program leads); largest segment Organic (27.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 65%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 55% (below 61% for the level by 6 pts); Navigational 37% (above 30% for the level by 7 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 12% (below 22% for the level by 10 pts); Navigational 65% (above 45% for the level by 20 pts); B2B 23% (below 33% for the level by 10 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 2.6% of total graduate inquiries (1,416 of 53,512); 1.9% of level final enrollments (26 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.84% (below total graduate 2.51% by 0.67 pts); B2B concentration 48% vs 61% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 27.5% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is B2B (47.5% of 40 enrolled students; 19 in B2B); 13 pts below the total graduate share for B2B (60.9%); 13 pts below enrolled-student baseline for B2B (60.9%); top B2B programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (784), MA in Health Care Administration (153), MA in Organizational Management (89), Master of Human Resource Management (60), Master of Information Systems Management (60); this program ranks #11 for B2B.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 40 matriculants, median age 36 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 62.5% female, in line with the graduate share (61.3%) (index 102 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is White (42.5%), in line with the graduate norm for White (42.8%); line of business skews to B2B (47.5%), 13 pts below typical graduate mix for B2B (60.9%); Core share is 27.5% (10 pts above graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (37.5% of matriculants).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MA in Special Education generated 1,819 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (up 53% vs Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking above median (#10 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 23 final enrollments (1.26% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (72.6% of program leads); largest segment Display (29.6% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 39%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 73% (above 61% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 27% (below 30% for the level by 3 pts); B2B 1% (below 9% for the level by 8 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 56% (above 22% for the level by 35 pts); Navigational 39% (below 45% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 4% (below 33% for the level by 29 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 3.4% of total graduate inquiries (1,819 of 53,512); 1.7% of level final enrollments (23 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.26% (below total graduate 2.51% by 1.25 pts); Core concentration 70% vs 18% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 70.4% vs 17.9% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (70.4% of 27 enrolled students; 19 in Core); 53 pts above the total graduate share for Core (17.9%); 53 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (17.9%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: MA in Psychology (73), Master of Business Administration (67), MA in Human Services (40), MA in Education (32), MA in Health Care Administration (29); this program ranks #9 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 27 matriculants, median age 46 is 9 pts above the graduate average (37); 92.6% female, 31 pts above the graduate share (61.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (44.4%), 17 pts above the graduate norm for Black (27.3%); line of business skews to Core (70.4%), 53 pts above typical graduate mix for Core (17.9%); Core share is 70.4% (53 pts above graduate Core at 17.9%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (37.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 2 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (15 stayed on-program; net +11.8% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

DPS in Organizational Leadership generated 650 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (no prior-period baseline in Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking lower volume (#19 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 0 final enrollments (0.00% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (50.9% of program leads); largest segment Organic (41.8% of leads). Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 51% (below 61% for the level by 10 pts); Navigational 41% (above 30% for the level by 11 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 1.2% of total graduate inquiries (650 of 53,512); 0.0% of level final enrollments (0 of 1,343); inquiry-to-enrollment 0.00% (below total graduate 2.51% by 2.51 pts).

Program summary

MPS in Leadership generated 185 inquiries in Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 (no prior-period baseline in Jul 2024 – Jun 2025), ranking lower volume (#22 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 0 final enrollments (0.00% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.84%).

Lead sources: Paid (42.2% of program leads); largest segment Organic (48.1% of leads). Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 42% (below 61% for the level by 19 pts); Navigational 41% (above 30% for the level by 11 pts); B2B 17% (above 9% for the level by 8 pts).

Appendix: Methodology & data dictionary

Reference for how metrics in this report are sourced, calculated, and labeled. Generated July 06, 2026. Pull scripts live in the project root; JSON artifacts feed generate_full_report.py.

1. Data sources & time windows

BigQuery — lead funnel

Project / dataset
advertising-data-mart.inquiries
Primary view
vw_lead_extract_details — one row per inquiry; program keyed on program_id at inquiry time.
Date field
inquiry_date (America/New_York calendar days; end dates exclusive).
Primary window (matrix KPIs, widgets, Sankey)
Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 — script: pull_full_data.py, pull_program_detail_widgets.py, pull_sankey_flow.py
Prior window (year-over-year comparison)
Jul 2024 – Jun 2025 — same programs, prior Jul–Jun year in program_data_full.json (py_* fields).
Monthly detail (all program detail pages)
Apr 2025 – Jun 2026 — 15 months (tail of prior YoY + current YoY); program_data_full.json → monthly keyed by program.
Program migration (all enrolling programs)
Jul 2025 – Jun 2026 inquiry window (fixed, not rolling) — pull_program_migration.py → program_migration.json.
Mix & program change analysis (standalone)
generate_program_insights.py → deploy/program-insights.html — portfolio mix YoY and program volume movers; not embedded in program detail profiles.

SQL Server — enrolled students & program bridge

Demographics
dbo.StudentRevenueMaster matriculations in the 12-month demographics window (DEMOGRAPHICS_MATRIC_* in report_periods.py), joined via dbo.program_srm_bridge. % Pell among Core LOB; % military funding among Military LOB; transfer credits undergraduate only; dependents excluded.
Enrollment line of business (widgets)
Same SRM matric window; LOB from lineofbusiness on enrolled students.
Account group (matrix column)
account_group from bridge table / data/program_srm_bridge.json (built with scripts/build_program_bridge.py from dbo.vw_program).

Artifact files consumed by the HTML report

  • program_data_full.json — matrix totals, prior period, monthly series
  • program_detail_widgets.json — marketing mix, Paid segment1 breakdown, enrollment LOB
  • program_sankey_flow.json — segment1 inflow + funnel stages
  • program_demographics.json — SRM profile + US regions + degree-level baselines
  • portfolio_mix_yoy.json — feeds standalone insights report only
  • program_migration.json — inquiry ↔ applied program flows (enrolling programs)
  • data/program_srm_bridge.json — program metadata and account groups
  • data/program_alignment.json — college, division, department, and academic leadership by program (from Program Alignment List workbook; scripts/build_program_alignment.py)

2. Metrics & formulas

Lead funnel flags (BigQuery)

  • Leads — COUNT(*) inquiries in window.
  • Apps Started — SUM(is_app_started).
  • Submitted — SUM(is_app_submitted) (monthly table only).
  • Decisions — SUM(is_appin) (application-in / decision stage).
  • Final enrollments — SUM(is_new_enrollment_final); used for matrix Enrl, Sankey funnel, and navigational %.

Summary matrix

  • Prior % change — (current − prior) / prior × 100; shown as ▲/▼/► when change exceeds ±5%; “NEW” if prior is zero.
  • % Core — share of final enrollments with LOB label Core (widget/SRM LOB buckets).
  • % Military — SRM lineofbusiness = Military when demographics exist; else widget LOB bucket Military.
  • % B2B — FTG + TB: SRM Full Tuition Grant + Tuition Benefit (displayed FTG / TB); else same labels in widget LOB.
  • % Navig — navigational_enrollments ÷ new_enrollments × 100 (enrollment mix, not lead mix). Navigational paths: legacy values mapped to marketing rollup Navigational, or null legacy with rollup Brand - Search / Organic.
  • Med Age / % Female — median age_at_matric and female % from SRM (male/female only, renormalized).
  • Net migration — enrollment view: inflow to this applied program minus outflow to other programs (12-month window on program detail pages).
  • Sort order — new_enrollments descending; level total row first, undecided rollup last (undergraduate tab).
  • Green highlight (top 5) — per tab, five highest values among enrolling programs for % Core, % Military, and % B2B (Undecided rollup excluded from ranking).

Detail widgets & charts

  • Leads — marketing rollup — inquiry counts by Paid / Navigational / B2B (resolve_marketing_levels).
  • Enrollments — marketing rollup — same hierarchy on rows with is_new_enrollment_final = 1.
  • Paid details — Paid-only leads and enrollments broken out by initial_marketing_segment1 (segment1).
  • Sankey inflow — segment1 on primary-window inquiries; funnel stages from program_data_full.json funnel block.
  • Monthly Enroll % — new_enrollments ÷ leads per month; App Start % and Decision % use the same lead denominator.
  • 4-month rolling average — mean of leads for months i−3…i; plotted from month 4 onward.
  • Geography index — round(program_region_% ÷ baseline_region_% × 100); baseline = all enrolled students at same degree level (Undergrad or Grad).
  • Demographic “index” callouts — plain-language point difference vs baseline (e.g. “5 pts above”), not the regional index formula.

LOB display labels (report UI)

SRM / widget labelShown as
CoreCore
Full Tuition GrantFTG
Tuition BenefitTB
MilitaryMilitary

3. Marketing segment mappings

Source field: mars_segment_legacy (≈ initial marketing segment on the inquiry). Hierarchy columns match business rollup used in widgets and Sankey. Unmapped legacy + rollup combinations are excluded from segment charts.

mars_segment_legacyMarketing rollupSegment rollupinitial_marketing_segment1
AP - EventsB2BOrganicOrganic B2B
AP - OrganicB2BOrganicOrganic B2B
Agg - Tier 1PaidAffiliateAffiliate
B2B - TPAPaidDisplayDisplay
Call InNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C
Display - FacebookPaidDisplayDisplay
Display - OtherPaidDisplayDisplay
Display - Partner DisplayPaidAffiliate - SearchAffiliate - Search
EP - EventsB2BOrganicOrganic B2B
EP - OrganicB2BOrganicOrganic B2B
Mil - OutreachNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C
OrganicNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C
Paid List - No ConsentNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C
ReferralNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C
Search - GenericPaidNon Brand - SearchNon-Brand Search
Search - TradenameNavigationalBrand - SearchBrand - Search
UnknownPaidDisplayDisplay
oapNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C

When mars_segment_legacy is null, mapping uses BigQuery marketing_segment_rollup:

ConditionMarketing rollupSegment rollupsegment1
null legacy + AffiliatePaidAffiliateAffiliate
null legacy + Affiliate - SearchPaidAffiliate - SearchAffiliate - Search
null legacy + Brand - SearchNavigationalBrand - SearchBrand - Search
null legacy + DisplayPaidDisplayDisplay
null legacy + Non Brand - SearchPaidNon Brand - SearchNon-Brand Search
null legacy + OrganicNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C

4. US region mapping (enrollment geography)

Student state from SRM → USPS abbreviation → region bucket. Map sketch shows % of program enrollments per region; callout index uses the formula in section 2.

  • Southwest: AZ, CO, NM, NV, OK, TX, UT
  • Southeast: AL, AR, FL, GA, KY, LA, MS, NC, SC, TN, VA, WV
  • West: AK, CA, HI, ID, MT, OR, WA, WY
  • Northeast: CT, DC, DE, MA, MD, ME, NH, NJ, NY, PA, RI, VT
  • Midwest: IA, IL, IN, KS, MI, MN, MO, ND, NE, OH, SD, WI
  • Other:

5. Cursor Agent build context

This report was built iteratively in Cursor using the Agent (Composer) with project rules in AGENTS.md, workspace rules under .cursor/rules/, and Python pull/generate scripts in the repo root. The HTML is static output from generate_full_report.py; data refreshes via BigQuery/SQL pull scripts.

Agent skills (project)

SkillRoleHow it was used
sql-queryPrimaryBigQuery and SQL Server patterns for lead funnel, program metrics, and enrollment pulls (vw_lead_extract_details, SRM).
uagc-brandPrimaryOfficial UAGC colors, typography (Montserrat), and editorial rules applied to report CSS and chart ramps.
ui-ux-pro-maxReferenceDesign-system lookup (styles, palettes, chart guidance); stub in repo pending full package from team.

Additional global Cursor skills (SDK, create-rule, canvas, code-review, etc.) are installed in the IDE but were secondary to the project skills above for this deliverable.

MCP servers

ServerRoleToolsUse in this report
user-bigqueryPrimary dataexecute-query, list-tables, describe-tableLead funnel, Sankey segment mix, marketing widgets, matrix pulls.
user-mssqlPrimary dataexecute_sqlStudentRevenueMaster demographics, LOB, program bridge (CVue codes).
user-gitDeploystatus, diff, commit, log, branchGitHub Pages deploy repo (<code>uagc-program-report</code>).
user-fetchSupportingfetchLanding-page checks and live URL validation.
user-filesystemSupportingread/write file, list directoryArtifact and report file management.
user-memorySupportingknowledge graphWorkspace facts and preferences across sessions.
user-sequential-thinkingSupportingsequentialthinkingStructured reasoning on complex pipeline changes.
user-timeSupportingget_current_time, convert_timePeriod labels and timezone alignment (America/New_York).
user-google-ads-mcpAvailablesearch, list_accessible_customersNot required for this static report; enabled in workspace.
plugin-slack-slackAvailableSlack integrationNot used in report generation.

Token & time summary

Pulled from Cursor usage export usage-events-2026-05-27.csv (2026-05-20 – 2026-05-27, UTC). Figures are agent events only (not IDE autocomplete). Refresh by exporting from the Cursor usage dashboard and saving as data/cursor_usage_events.csv or updating the file in Downloads.

MetricValue
Agent events120
Total tokens88,295,014 (88.3M)
Agent sessions12 (gap > 45 min)
Estimated active time16.1 hours (session span 14.5h + padding)
Work phaseTokens%Est. hoursSessions
Environment & initial build (through May 22)29.6M34%10.79
Report refine & deploy (May 26–27)45.9M52%5.43
Other days in export (e.g. May 20)12.8M14%0.00

Estimated active time = span between first and last agent event per session (>45 min gap starts a new session) plus 8 minutes padding per session. Source: Cursor dashboard usage export (agent events).

Models (by token volume): composer-2.5-fast (51.0M), claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking (37.3M)
